title: Aster cuneatus
intro: Aster cuneatus, also known as wedge-leaved aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aster cuneatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Aster cuneatus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicines, to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Aster cuneatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America and is a member of the genus Aster. The genus Aster is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as asters or Michaelmas daisies. Within the family Asteraceae, Aster cuneatus is part of the subfamily Asteroideae. The plant's classification is further organized into the order Asterales.
